# Lab Walkthroughs

Hands-on walkthroughs that exercise the Straylight VMs directly. Each lab pairs 1:1 with an external guide (the guide teaches the concept; the lab has you type the keystrokes against this infrastructure) and carries a self-check quiz plus a module exam.

**→ [INDEX.md](INDEX.md) — deep-linked lab navigator.** Generated by [`gen-index.py`](gen-index.py) — rerun it after adding or editing labs.

## Current catalog: example module

The repo currently ships one representative module while the full catalog undergoes hands-on verification, lab by lab:

### AD CS functional test — `adcs-functest-*` · needs `LAB_PROFILE=ad-cs-two-tier`

The operational acceptance test for the lab's core PKI — proves the deployed two-tier AD CS hierarchy works end to end. The labs are numbered in run order (Lab 3 issues the certificate Lab 4 revokes):

| Lab | What it proves |
|---|---|
| [1 — Service health](labs/adcs-functest-1-service-health-walkthrough.md) | CA service state, audit events (4886 request / 4887 issued / 4872 CRL published), database and backup posture |
| [2 — Connectivity](labs/adcs-functest-2-connectivity-walkthrough.md) | Every enrollment interface answers: DCOM/RPC, LDAP CDP/AIA objects, HTTP CDP, `certutil -ping` |
| [3 — Issuance](labs/adcs-functest-3-issuance-walkthrough.md) | A certificate request travels the full path: template → CSR → submit → issue → chain validation |
| [4 — Revocation](labs/adcs-functest-4-revocation-walkthrough.md) | Revoke → CRL publish → client verification actually fails (with the real 26-week base + 1-day delta CRL windows) |

Each lab links its quiz; the module exam is [exams/2026-05-12-adcs-functest-exam.md](exams/2026-05-12-adcs-functest-exam.md) and the design spec is [specs/2026-05-12-adcs-functest-labs-design.md](specs/2026-05-12-adcs-functest-labs-design.md).

## The full catalog

80+ labs across ~22 modules (revocation, ACME/HTTP-01/DNS-01 automation, CAA, mTLS, failure scenarios, code signing, Java keystores, OpenSSL FIPS, webserver TLS, template flags, PKI automation, a NIST SP 800-52 track, and the forward-looking PQC suite) have been through a multi-agent correctness review and return here module-by-module as each passes hands-on verification. Until then they live in the development archive (branch `content/walkthroughs-unverified`).

See [STRAYLIGHT-REFERENCE.md](STRAYLIGHT-REFERENCE.md) for the VM/profile/IP ground truth the labs build on.

## AZ-700 track — Azure Network Engineer

A second track (`az700-labs/` + quizzes/exams/specs, indexed in its own INDEX
section) covering the full AZ-700 blueprint across 7 modules / 29 labs: core
VNets/routing, DNS, hybrid connectivity (the Straylight lab plays the on-prem
site over a real site-to-site VPN), load balancing, Private Link, network
security, and monitoring. Labs deploy real Azure resources per session —
`azure/scripts/az700.sh deploy <slug>` before, `destroy` after, single-digit
dollars per session; expensive topics (ExpressRoute, DDoS, Firewall Premium)
are explicit paper labs. Prerequisites, cost model, and safety rules:
[azure/README.md](../../azure/README.md); naming/addressing conventions:
[STRAYLIGHT-REFERENCE.md](STRAYLIGHT-REFERENCE.md#azure-conventions-az-700-track).

Like the main catalog, AZ-700 modules land here module-by-module as they pass
hands-on verification (az-CLI steps are walkverify-annotated with `host=lab`);
until then they live on `content/walkthroughs-unverified`.

## Host placement for CA labs

Steps that **administer the CA** — any `certutil -config <ca>` operation
(`-view`, `-revoke`, `-CRL`, `-SetCATemplates`) and `certreq -submit` — must run
`host=<ca>` (e.g. `issueca`). Remote DCOM to the CA fails on the shared-NAT lab
topology (`RPC_S_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE`: every VM shares NAT IP `10.0.2.15`, so the
CA's RPC endpoint is advertised on an address that resolves back to the client;
no host-side fix works). **Relying-party** steps (`certutil -verify -urlfetch`,
url-cache) run `host=<client>` (e.g. `manage1`). Cross-host artifacts (an issued
cert a client must verify) move over **SMB** (`\\<ca>\C$…`), never DCOM. The
`adcs-functest` revocation lab is the reference example.
